title: lactuca sylvestris var. integrifolia
intro: Lactuca sylvestris var. integrifolia, also known as wild lettuce, is a perennial plant in the dandelion family with lanceolate leaves and white or yellowish flowers. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its content of lactucarium, a milky sap extracted from the plant's leaves. The medicinal properties of wild lettuce make it a valuable herbal remedy for treating insomnia, anxiety, and restlessness, as well as providing relief from pain and inflammation. Wild lettuce can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
